hypothesis: If a 426M-parameter hybrid Transformer–Mamba encoder–decoder is pre-trained with a shared tokenizer across multiple molecular textual modalities using masked language modeling for alignment and decoder reconstruction, then it will learn a unified, chemically meaningful latent space that yields better clustering, higher property-prediction accuracy across small molecules, polymers, and electrolytes, and more valid/faithful inter-representation translation than unimodal or non-hybrid baselines because multimodal objectives capture modality-invariant chemical structure.
